data.title: Ripple Enhances XRP Ledger Security with AI-Driven Measures
data.description: Ripple has introduced a comprehensive security upgrade for the XRP Ledger, incorporating artificial intelligence to bolster its defenses. According to NS3.AI, the initiative includes AI-assisted code scanning for every pull request and automated adversarial testing throughout the development process. Ripple's newly established red team has already identified over ten bugs, underscoring the effectiveness of these measures. The upcoming release of the XRP Ledger will prioritize bug fixes and improvements, foregoing the introduction of new features.

data.title: Circle Unfreezes ckUSDC and Additional Wallets
data.description: Circle has recently unfrozen ckUSDC (Dfinity Bridge) and two other hot wallets starting with 0x00e, according to Foresight News. Blockchain investigator ZachXBT reported this development on social media, noting that five out of the 16 hot wallets he previously mentioned have now been unfrozen. ZachXBT also indicated that once a civil case in New York is unsealed, he plans to publicly identify the plaintiff, experts, and all other involved parties.

data.title: Bitdeer maintains zero Bitcoin holdings, producing and selling 146 BTC this week.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed mining company Bitdeer disclosed its latest Bitcoin holdings data. As of the week ending March 28, the company mined 146 BTC and sold all of its output, currently maintaining a zero Bitcoin holding status.

data.title: Iran's Red Crescent Reports Extensive Damage from Recent Conflict
data.description: On March 28, according to Jin10, the Iranian Red Crescent reported that the ongoing conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran has resulted in significant damage to over 92,000 civilian facilities in Iran as of March 27. The statistics reveal that more than 71,000 residential properties and over 20,000 commercial facilities have been affected nationwide. In the capital, Tehran, approximately 30,000 residential and commercial units have suffered damage. Additionally, 600 schools have been impacted by the attacks. The rescue infrastructure has also been affected, with damage reported to 290 medical and emergency facilities, including 17 Red Crescent branches. Furthermore, 46 ambulances, 3 rescue helicopters, and 48 other operational vehicles have sustained damage.

data.title: CLARITY Act Revisions Aim to Strengthen Legal Protections for DeFi Developers
data.description: Cynthia Lummis announced that the latest revisions to the CLARITY Act are designed to offer enhanced legal protection for developers in the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector and those working on non-custodial software. According to NS3.AI, these changes aim to address some of the legal uncertainties faced by developers in these areas. However, Jake Chervinsky noted that the language concerning money transmission still requires further clarification. The revised bill is anticipated to be reviewed by the Senate Banking Committee in April.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Market Strategy Amid Uncertain Conditions
data.description:
On March 28, Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua shared insights on social media regarding his market strategy. According to BlockBeats, Yi mentioned setting a profit-taking line and maintaining a small position for easier management. He noted that the market rebound has not met expectations and that the ongoing conflict has not concluded in the short term, indicating a wait for new buying opportunities.

Given the recent market downturn, Yi's comments may suggest a bearish profit-taking approach.

data.title: US Senator: The Clarity Act will provide stronger protection for DeFi developers.
data.description: U.S. Senator Cynthia Lummis stated that the latest revisions to the Clarity Act will provide "the strongest protection" for DeFi and developers. Responding to concerns raised by crypto lawyer Jake Chervinsky regarding the developer liability clauses in the draft bill, she said that both parties have adjusted the relevant content to strengthen legal protections for developers of non-custodial software. However, Chervinsky pointed out that the current draft's definition of fund transfers could still expose some DeFi developers to liability risks, and the relevant clauses are not yet fully clear. The report states that the latest revised version of the bill has not yet been released and is expected to proceed to the Senate Banking Committee for review in April. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Brother Machi opened a new long position in HYPE with 10x leverage, and the overall position turned from profitable to loss.
data.description: According to Hyperbot data, Huang Licheng, known as "Brother Machi," opened a new long position in HYPE with 10x leverage this morning, currently holding 9,000 HYPE. In addition, he also holds a long position in Ethereum with 25x leverage (currently holding 3,975 ETH) and a long position in Bitcoin with 40x leverage (currently holding 33 BTC), with a total position value of approximately $10.442 million. His position has turned from profitable to loss, currently showing a floating loss of approximately $248,000.

data.title: Hyperbot Data Reveals High-Leverage Trades by Huang Licheng
data.description: Hyperbot data indicates that Huang Licheng has initiated a 10x leverage long position on HYPE, currently holding 9,000 HYPE tokens. According to Odaily, he also maintains a 25x leverage long position on Ethereum, holding 3,975 ETH, and a 40x leverage long position on Bitcoin, holding 33 BTC. The total value of these positions is approximately $10.442 million, which has shifted from profit to a current unrealized loss of about $248,000.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Extended U.S. Homeland Security Shutdown
data.description: Polymarket has estimated a 75% probability that the U.S. Department of Homeland Security shutdown will extend beyond 60 days. According to NS3.AI, this marks a 37% increase in just 24 hours following the House's approval of a temporary funding bill, which has now been forwarded to the Senate. The trading volume for this event contract has surpassed $1.1 million. However, the bill is not anticipated to pass in the Senate.

data.title: Thailand and Iran Reach Agreement on Hormuz Strait Passage
data.description: Thailand and Iran have reached an agreement allowing ships to pass through the Hormuz Strait. According to Odaily, the deal was finalized on March 28, facilitating maritime navigation in the strategic waterway.

data.title: U.S. House Passes Temporary Homeland Security Funding Bill Amid Shutdown Concerns
data.description:
The U.S. House of Representatives has passed a temporary funding bill aimed at ending the government shutdown, sending it to the Senate for consideration. According to Odaily, the probability of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) shutdown lasting more than 48 days has risen to 92%, a 31% increase in 24 hours. The likelihood of the shutdown extending beyond 52 days is now at 54%, up 12%, and the chance of it continuing for over 60 days has climbed to 75%, a 37% increase in the same period. The event contract's trading volume has exceeded $1.1 million.

The bill provides funding for each DHS agency at current levels until May 22, marking the latest attempt to end the partial government shutdown that began on February 14. It passed the House with a vote of 213-203, with three Democrats joining Republicans in support. However, the bill is not expected to pass in the Senate, potentially prolonging the partial government shutdown. Before passing the House version, the Senate's version of the funding agreement was rejected. House Speaker Johnson stated that Republicans would not support any measures to reopen the border or halt immigration enforcement.

data.title: U.S. Spot Ethereum ETF Experiences Continued Net Outflows
data.description: The U.S. spot Ethereum ETF has seen a net outflow of $48.5 million, marking the eighth consecutive trading day of net outflows. According to ChainCatcher, this trend highlights ongoing investor sentiment and market dynamics surrounding Ethereum ETFs in the United States.

data.title: Polymarket reports that the probability of the U.S. Department of Homeland Security shutdown lasting more than 60 days has risen to 75%, a 37% increase in the past 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the U.S. House of Representatives has passed a temporary Homeland Security funding bill aimed at ending the government shutdown and has sent it to the Senate. Polymarket's probability of a Department of Homeland Security shutdown lasting more than 48 days has risen to 92%, a 31% increase in 24 hours; the probability of a shutdown lasting more than 52 days has risen to 54%, a 12% increase in 24 hours; and the probability of a shutdown lasting more than 60 days has risen to 75%, a 37% increase in 24 hours. As of now, the contract trading volume for this event has exceeded $1.1 million. The bill reportedly provides each agency under the Department of Homeland Security with funding at current levels until May 22, representing the latest attempt to end the partial government shutdown that began on February 14. The bill passed the House with 213 votes in favor and 203 against, with three Democratic members voting in favor and the rest being Republicans. However, the bill is not expected to pass the Senate, potentially prolonging the partial government shutdown deadlock. They rejected the Senate version of the appropriations agreement before passing the House version. House Speaker Johnson stated that Republicans would not participate in any measures to reopen borders or halt immigration enforcement. Odaily Seer continues to monitor prediction markets, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Thailand and Iran have reached an agreement allowing ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: On March 28, Thailand and Iran reached an agreement allowing ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: South Korea Nominates Shin Hyun Song as Central Bank Governor Candidate
data.description: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung has nominated Shin Hyun Song, head of the Monetary and Economic Department at the Bank for International Settlements, as a candidate for the new governor of the Bank of Korea. According to ChainCatcher, Shin is known for his pragmatic and hawkish stance. He has expressed skepticism towards the government's push for a stablecoin pegged to the Korean won. Shin argues that in countries with volatile exchange rates and susceptibility to capital outflows, stablecoins can easily become tools for capital flight. He believes that stablecoins linked to domestic currency might accelerate this trend.

data.title: Trader Reflects on Challenges and Market Trends in 2026
data.description:
On March 28, trader Eugene Ng Ah Sio announced on his personal channel that he has exited all positions, stating, "I tried, but it didn't pay off. 2026 has proven to be more challenging than 2025, indicating a need to reduce trading frequency. It seems more patience is required."

According to BlockBeats, on March 16, Eugene Ng Ah Sio had previously shared that he had shifted to a bullish stance. Despite the overall weakening of global risk assets, the crypto market showed resilience, marking the first relative strength since Bitcoin's crash from $60,000. Ng noted that although he did not buy at the lowest point, he preferred to follow strong buying after breaking out of consolidation to better manage risk. He observed that several altcoins exhibited natural rounded bottom patterns, suggesting a higher probability of short-term gains in the crypto market. Ng speculated that if Bitcoin effectively breaks through $74,000, the entire crypto market could see a significant rally, with major coins like ETH and SOL potentially returning to their previous trading ranges of $2,400 and $100, respectively.

data.title: Trader Eugene: Has exited the market with a stop-loss order and plans to reduce trading frequency.
data.description: Trader Eugene posted that all his positions have been closed at a loss. The trade failed to achieve the expected return, and he noted that the market environment in 2026 will be more challenging than in 2025. He will consider reducing his trading frequency and exercising more patience going forward.

data.title: Ripple CEO Predicts Stablecoins as Key to Crypto's Future Growth
data.description:
Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse has highlighted stablecoins as a pivotal development for businesses seeking faster and more efficient payment solutions. According to Cointelegraph, Garlinghouse emphasized that many companies are actively exploring strategies to integrate stablecoins into their operations. He noted that corporate leaders, including boards of directors and CEOs from Fortune 500 and Fortune 2000 companies, are inquiring about stablecoin adoption with their financial officers. Garlinghouse described this interest as a significant opportunity for treasurers and CFOs, likening it to a 'ChatGPT moment' for the crypto sector, as it could serve as a gateway for businesses to access a wider array of blockchain-based services.

Bloomberg Intelligence has projected that stablecoin flows could grow at a compounded annual rate of 80%, potentially reaching $56.6 trillion by 2030. This growth would position stablecoins as a crucial payment tool in global finance. Garlinghouse pointed out that stablecoins processed over $33 trillion in trading volume last year, with the majority coming from Tether’s USDt and Circle’s USDC. Ripple has also entered the stablecoin market with the launch of Ripple USD (RLUSD) in December 2024, which has become the 10th largest stablecoin by market cap, valued at $1.4 billion according to CoinGecko data.

Ripple has bolstered its blockchain payments infrastructure through strategic acquisitions, including the purchase of institutional-based prime brokerage Hidden Road for $1.25 billion and corporate treasury platform GTreasury for $1 billion. Garlinghouse expressed optimism about Ripple's performance, indicating that the company is poised for a 'record quarter' and has been experiencing significant growth since these acquisitions.

Garlinghouse also discussed the potential impact of the CLARITY Act on the crypto industry. He believes that stablecoin payments and broader blockchain adoption could be accelerated if the legislation passes Congress and is enacted into law. He stressed the importance of clear U.S. regulation to prevent policy from being used for political purposes, referencing concerns about regulatory approaches that may not align with the best interests of the United States.

data.title: Trader Eugene Exits Positions Amid Challenging Market Conditions
data.description: Trader Eugene has announced that he has exited all his positions due to stop-loss triggers. According to Odaily, Eugene noted that the recent trades did not yield the expected returns and highlighted that the market environment in 2026 is more challenging compared to 2025. Moving forward, Eugene plans to reduce trading frequency and exercise greater patience.
